steps.title: Testing choice B
steps.content:
Backsolving — testing choice B (c=10(d+11)):
Plug in d=0 days:
$$c = 10(0+11) = 110$$
But the expected cost for zero days is just the insurance fee of 10 dollars.
Since 110 ≠ 10, choice B fails.

steps.title: Testing choice C
steps.content:
Fails. Testing choice C (c=11d+10):
Plug in d=0 days:
$$c = 11·0 + 10 = 10$$
This matches the insurance fee of 10 dollars for zero days.
Testing another value, say d=1 day:
$$c = 11·1 + 10 = 21$$
Expected cost: 11 dollars for one day plus 10 dollars insurance = 21 dollars.
Since both checks succeed, choice C is correct.
That matches choice C — C is correct.
